- Run all quality gates

## Steps

1. Define SubdomainClassification enum (CORE, SUPPORTING, GENERIC) and domain value objects (DomainStory, BoundedContext, ContextRelationship, AggregateDesign) in `src/domain/models/domain_values.py`.
2. Implement UbiquitousLanguage entity in `src/domain/models/ubiquitous_language.py` — manages term glossary with add_term(), get_terms_for_context(), find_ambiguous_terms().
3. Implement DomainModel aggregate root in `src/domain/models/domain_model.py` — commands: add_domain_story, add_term, add_bounded_context, classify_subdomain, design_aggregate, finalize. Enforce all 5 invariants in finalize().
4. Define DomainModelGenerated event in `src/domain/events/domain_events.py`.
5. Define ArtifactGenerationPort and FileWriterPort Protocols in `src/application/ports/`.
6. Implement question-to-artifact mapping in ArtifactGenerationHandler — maps each question's answers to the appropriate DomainModel command (Q1 -> add actors, Q2 -> add entities, etc. per the mapping table in the consolidated research).
7. Implement markdown renderers for PRD, DDD.md, ARCHITECTURE.md — each renderer takes a DomainModel and produces a string matching the corresponding template structure.
8. Wire CLI adapter as thin Typer command calling ArtifactGenerationHandler.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] DomainModel aggregate enforces all 5 invariants from DDD.md Section 5
- [ ] finalize() emits DomainModelGenerated event carrying the complete model
- [ ] Question-to-artifact mapping correctly transforms all 10 question answers into DomainModel commands
- [ ] PRD.md output matches the structure defined in docs/templates/PRD_TEMPLATE.md
- [ ] DDD.md output includes: domain stories, ubiquitous language glossary, subdomain classification, bounded context map, aggregate designs
- [ ] ARCHITECTURE.md output includes: layer structure informed by subdomain classification (hexagonal for Core, layered for Supporting, ACL for Generic)
- [ ] Ambiguous terms (same word in multiple contexts) are documented with per-context meanings
- [ ] Core subdomains have aggregate designs with invariants; Supporting/Generic do not require them
- [ ] Domain layer has zero external dependencies
- [ ] All model names match DDD.md ubiquitous language exactly
- [ ] >= 90% test coverage on domain layer, >= 80% overall

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Term not in any story | add_term("Widget") but no story mentions Widget | finalize() raises InvariantViolation("Term 'Widget' not found in any domain story") |
| Context without classification | add_bounded_context("Payments") without classify_subdomain("Payments", ...) | finalize() raises InvariantViolation("BoundedContext 'Payments' has no classification") |
| Core without aggregate | classify_subdomain("Discovery", CORE) but no design_aggregate() | finalize() raises InvariantViolation("Core subdomain 'Discovery' has no aggregate design") |
| One-way relationship | A -> B exists but B does not reference A | finalize() raises InvariantViolation("Relationship A->B is not bidirectional") |
| Ambiguous term undocumented | "Config" used in Bootstrap and Tool Translation without per-context definitions | finalize() raises InvariantViolation("Ambiguous term 'Config' needs per-context definitions") |
| Empty discovery answers | DiscoveryCompleted with all empty answers | Handler raises ValueError("No substantive answers to generate artifacts from") |
| Duplicate story name | add_domain_story() with same name twice | Raise DuplicateStoryError |
| Quick mode (5 questions) | Only MVP questions answered | Generate artifacts from available data; mark sections as "needs elaboration" where data is thin |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes (zero errors)
- [ ] Type check passes (zero errors)
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% overall coverage
- [ ] Domain layer tests have >= 90% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- **Blocked by k7m.17** (alty guide) — needs DiscoveryCompleted event as input
- **Blocks k7m.19** (fitness generation) — FitnessTestSuite reads BoundedContextMap and SubdomainClassification from DomainModel
- **Blocks k7m.20** (ticket generation) — TicketPlan reads DomainModel artifacts
- **Blocks k7m.21** (config generation) — ToolConfig reads DomainModel for ubiquitous language and context rules
- **Risk:** Template structure may evolve — use FileWriterPort Protocol so rendering is decoupled from file I/O
- **Risk:** Question-to-artifact mapping may produce incomplete artifacts when users give sparse answers — design renderers to mark thin sections rather than fail

title: Implement alty generate fitness (import-linter + pytestarch)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Implement `alty generate fitness` — the command that generates executable architecture fitness function tests (import-linter contracts + pytestarch rules) from the bounded context map and subdomain classification. Without this, architectural constraints are documented but not enforced, and bounded context boundaries erode over time as developers add cross-context imports.

## Background / Context

- Spike research: `docs/research/20260222_python_architecture_testing_fitness_functions.md` — evaluates import-linter and pytestarch, defines contract types (layers, forbidden, independence, acyclic_siblings).
- DDD.md Section 3, Complexity Budget treatment levels: Core=strict (all 4 contract types), Supporting=moderate (layers + forbidden), Generic=minimal (single forbidden ACL boundary).
- DDD.md Section 5, FitnessTestSuite aggregate: Contains Contract, ArchRule, ContractStrictness. 5 invariants defined.
- CLI design: `docs/research/20260222_cli_mcp_design.md` — `alty generate fitness` maps to FitnessGenerationPort, FitnessTestSuite aggregate.
- **Pattern reference:** Existing import-linter TOML format in pyproject.toml (alty's own project uses this pattern).

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Architecture Testing (CORE subdomain) |
| Ubiquitous Language | FitnessTestSuite, Contract, ArchRule, ContractStrictness, FitnessTestsGenerated |
| Layer | Domain (FitnessTestSuite aggregate + value objects) + Application (FitnessGenerationHandler) + Infrastructure (TOML writer + test file writer + CLI adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `FitnessTestSuite` | Aggregate Root | Complete set of architecture tests for a project — contracts and rules organized by bounded context |
| `Contract` | Entity | An import-linter contract: name, type (layers/forbidden/independence/acyclic_siblings), modules, configuration |
| `ArchRule` | Entity | A pytestarch rule: name, assertion (e.g., "modules in X should not import from Y"), bounded_context_id |
| `ContractStrictness` | Value Object (Enum) | STRICT (Core), MODERATE (Supporting), MINIMAL (Generic) — determines which contract types are generated |
| `FitnessTestsGenerated` | Domain Event | Emitted when approve() succeeds — carries the complete FitnessTestSuite |

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI (alty generate fitness)   FitnessGenerationHandler   FitnessTestSuite    FileWriterPort
        |                            |                        |                  |
        |-- generate(model_data) --->|                        |                  |
        |                            |-- create_suite() ----->|                  |
        |                            |   For each BC:         |                  |
        |                            |   classify_strictness->|                  |
        |                            |   generate_contracts ->|                  |
        |                            |<-- contracts list -----|                  |
        |<-- preview() --------------|                        |                  |
        |   (show TOML + test files) |                        |                  |
        |-- approve ----------------->|                        |                  |
        |                            |-- approve() ---------> |                  |
        |                            |<- FitnessTestsGenerated|                  |
        |                            |-- write TOML ---------------------->|
        |                            |-- write test files ----------------->|
        |<-- "Fitness tests done" ---|                        |                  |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | `FitnessTestSuite` owns contract generation logic and invariants; `FitnessGenerationHandler` orchestrates the pipeline; TOML writer and test file writer are separate infrastructure adapters |
| **O**pen/Closed | New contract types can be added by extending the ContractStrictness-to-contract-types mapping without modifying existing contract generation logic |
| **L**iskov Substitution | `FitnessGenerationPort` Protocol honored by CLI adapter and future MCP adapter |
| **I**nterface Segregation | `FitnessGenerationPort` (generate + preview + approve), `TomlWriterPort` (write import-linter config), `TestFileWriterPort` (write pytestarch files) are separate Protocols |
| **D**ependency Inversion | `FitnessGenerationHandler` depends on writer Protocols, not on concrete TOML/file implementations; domain logic computes contracts without knowing output format |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```python
# tests/domain/test_fitness_test_suite.py
def test_create_suite_empty():
    """New FitnessTestSuite has no contracts or rules."""

def test_generate_contracts_core_produces_all_four_types():
    """Core subdomain BC generates layers + forbidden + independence + acyclic_siblings contracts."""

def test_generate_contracts_supporting_produces_two_types():
    """Supporting subdomain BC generates layers + forbidden contracts only."""

def test_generate_contracts_generic_produces_one_type():
    """Generic subdomain BC generates single forbidden (ACL boundary) contract only."""

def test_every_bc_has_at_least_one_contract():
    """Invariant 1: Cannot approve suite if any BC has zero contracts."""

def test_core_has_all_four_contract_types():
    """Invariant 2: Core BC missing any of the 4 types raises InvariantViolation."""

def test_supporting_has_layers_and_forbidden():
    """Invariant 3: Supporting BC missing layers or forbidden raises InvariantViolation."""

def test_generic_has_forbidden():
    """Invariant 4: Generic BC missing forbidden raises InvariantViolation."""

def test_no_cross_context_module_references():
    """Invariant 5: Contract referencing modules outside its BC raises InvariantViolation."""

def test_preview_returns_human_readable_summary():
    """Preview shows contract count per BC, types, and strictness level."""

def test_approve_emits_fitness_tests_generated():
    """Successful approve() emits FitnessTestsGenerated event."""

def test_layers_contract_structure():
    """Layers contract specifies domain -> application -> infrastructure order."""

def test_forbidden_contract_structure():
    """Forbidden contract prevents domain from importing infrastructure."""

def test_independence_contract_structure():
    """Independence contract prevents direct imports between sibling BCs."""

def test_acyclic_siblings_contract_structure():
    """Acyclic siblings contract prevents circular dependencies between BCs."""
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_fitness_test_suite.py -v` — should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/fitness_values.py` — ContractStrictness enum (STRICT, MODERATE, MINIMAL), contract type constants
2. Create `src/domain/models/contract.py` — Contract entity with name, type, modules, bounded_context_id
3. Create `src/domain/models/arch_rule.py` — ArchRule entity with name, assertion, bounded_context_id
4. Create `src/domain/models/fitness_test_suite.py` — FitnessTestSuite aggregate root with generate_contracts(), preview(), approve(), all 5 invariants
5. Create `src/domain/events/fitness_events.py` — FitnessTestsGenerated event
6. Create `src/application/commands/fitness_generation_handler.py` — orchestrates reading DomainModel, building FitnessTestSuite, writing outputs
7. Create `src/application/ports/fitness_generation_port.py` — FitnessGenerationPort Protocol
8. Create `src/application/ports/toml_writer_port.py` — TomlWriterPort Protocol for import-linter config
9. Create `src/application/ports/test_file_writer_port.py` — TestFileWriterPort Protocol for pytestarch files

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_fitness_test_suite.py -v` — should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify contract generation logic matches the treatment levels from DDD.md Section 3
- Ensure FitnessTestSuite has no infrastructure dependencies
- Verify generated TOML matches import-linter's expected format
- Run all quality gates

## Steps

1. Define ContractStrictness enum and contract type constants in `src/domain/models/fitness_values.py`. Map STRICT -> [layers, forbidden, independence, acyclic_siblings], MODERATE -> [layers, forbidden], MINIMAL -> [forbidden].
2. Implement Contract entity in `src/domain/models/contract.py` — each contract knows its type, target modules, and which BC it belongs to.
3. Implement ArchRule entity in `src/domain/models/arch_rule.py` — pytestarch assertions that complement import-linter contracts.
4. Implement FitnessTestSuite aggregate root in `src/domain/models/fitness_test_suite.py`. The generate_contracts() command takes a BoundedContextMap + SubdomainClassification list and produces the correct contracts per BC based on strictness. Enforce all 5 invariants.
5. Define FitnessTestsGenerated event in `src/domain/events/fitness_events.py`.
6. Define Protocols: FitnessGenerationPort, TomlWriterPort, TestFileWriterPort.
7. Implement FitnessGenerationHandler — reads DomainModel (BoundedContextMap + classifications), builds FitnessTestSuite, previews, writes TOML and test files via ports.
8. Implement TOML rendering logic — generates `[tool.importlinter]` sections in pyproject.toml format. Generate pytestarch test file content per BC.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] FitnessTestSuite correctly maps SubdomainClassification to ContractStrictness (Core->STRICT, Supporting->MODERATE, Generic->MINIMAL)
- [ ] Core BCs generate all 4 contract types: layers, forbidden, independence, acyclic_siblings
- [ ] Supporting BCs generate 2 contract types: layers, forbidden
- [ ] Generic BCs generate 1 contract type: forbidden (ACL boundary only)
- [ ] All 5 invariants from DDD.md Section 5 are enforced
- [ ] Generated import-linter TOML is valid and matches import-linter's expected format
- [ ] Generated pytestarch test files are valid Python that can be run with pytest
- [ ] Layers contracts enforce domain -> application -> infrastructure dependency direction
- [ ] Forbidden contracts prevent domain layer from importing infrastructure
- [ ] Independence contracts prevent direct imports between sibling bounded contexts
- [ ] Preview shows human-readable summary before writing any files
- [ ] Domain layer has zero external dependencies
- [ ] >= 90% test coverage on domain layer, >= 80% overall

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Single BC project | DomainModel with only one bounded context | Generate contracts for that BC; skip independence and acyclic_siblings (no siblings) |
| BC with no modules yet | Bounded context defined but src/ directory empty | Generate contract with placeholder module paths; add comment "update paths after implementation" |
| Mixed classifications | 2 Core + 1 Supporting + 1 Generic BCs | Each gets correct strictness level; total contract count reflects the mix |
| Missing classification | BC without SubdomainClassification | Raise InvariantViolation("BC 'X' has no subdomain classification") |
| Cross-context reference | Contract references module from another BC | Raise InvariantViolation("Contract 'X' references module outside its bounded context") |
| Existing pyproject.toml | pyproject.toml already has [tool.importlinter] section | Merge new contracts; do not overwrite existing ones; warn about conflicts |
| Empty DomainModel | No bounded contexts defined | Raise ValueError("No bounded contexts to generate fitness tests for") |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes (zero errors)
- [ ] Type check passes (zero errors)
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% overall coverage
- [ ] Domain layer tests have >= 90% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- **Blocked by k7m.18** (artifact generation) — needs DomainModel with BoundedContextMap and SubdomainClassification as input
- **Risk:** import-linter TOML format may have edge cases not covered by the spike research — validate generated TOML against import-linter's actual parser in integration tests
- **Risk:** pytestarch API may differ from documented examples — add integration test that actually imports and runs a generated test
- **Dependency:** import-linter and pytestarch must be added to pyproject.toml dev dependencies

title: Spike: Guided DDD question framework
description:
## Research Question

What is the minimal effective set of DDD / Domain Storytelling questions to take a 4-5 sentence idea and produce bounded contexts, ubiquitous language, aggregate design, and subdomain classification — for both technical and non-technical users?

## Timebox
4 hours

## Background
- Users start with a simple README (4-5 sentences)
- We need to ask structured questions that progressively build DDD artifacts
- Questions must work for solo developers (no workshop facilitation)
- Must produce: domain stories, ubiquitous language glossary, subdomain classification, bounded contexts, aggregate boundaries
- **Complexity Budget (P0 feature from k7m.9):** During DDD discovery, each subdomain must be classified as Core/Supporting/Generic. This classification drives ticket detail level, fitness function strictness, and recommended implementation approach. The question framework must include subdomain classification questions.
- **5 Personas (from PRD):** Questions must work for all personas, including non-technical ones:
  - Solo Developer — technical, building from scratch
  - Team Lead — technical, adopting on existing codebase
  - AI Tool Switcher — technical, using multiple tools
  - **Product Owner** — non-technical, translating business vision into structured requirements
  - **Domain Expert** — non-technical (HR, sales, ops), capturing domain knowledge for developers
- Non-technical users will not understand DDD terminology. Questions for PO/Domain Expert personas must use plain business language while still producing valid DDD artifacts.

## Investigation Approach
1. Review Domain Storytelling methodology (Hofer & Schwentner)
2. Review Event Storming question flows
3. Identify minimum question set that produces useful DDD artifacts
4. Design question flow (order, dependencies, branching)
5. **Design subdomain classification questions** -- how to guide users to classify Core vs Supporting vs Generic, with examples and heuristics
6. **Design persona-adapted question paths** -- same questions reworded for non-technical users (PO, Domain Expert). Technical users get DDD terminology; non-technical users get business language equivalents.
7. Create example: apply questions to a sample project idea

## Expected Deliverables
- [ ] Research report at docs/research/YYYYMMDD_ddd_question_framework.md
- [ ] Ordered list of questions with rationale
- [ ] **Subdomain classification questions and heuristics** (Core/Supporting/Generic)
- [ ] **Complexity budget output format** (which subdomain gets which treatment level)
- [ ] **Persona-adapted question variants** (technical vs non-technical wording for same questions)
- [ ] Example application to a sample project
- [ ] Question-to-artifact mapping (which questions produce which DDD artifacts)
- [ ] Follow-up tickets for implementation
close_reason: All 8 deliverables complete: consolidated research report with 10-question dual-register framework, question-to-artifact mapping, subdomain classification flow (Khononov decision tree), complexity budget output format, persona-adapted question variants (technical + non-technical), PawWatch marketplace example application, and 6 follow-up implementation tickets documented.

title: Implement alty generate tickets (beads epics from DDD)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Implement `alty generate tickets` — the command that generates dependency-ordered beads epics and tickets from DDD artifacts. Without this, developers must manually create tickets from domain stories, guess dependency order, and decide ticket detail level. This automates the DDD-to-backlog pipeline with complexity-budget-driven two-tier generation: Core subdomains get full-detail tickets (AC, TDD phases, SOLID mapping, edge cases), Supporting get standard tickets, Generic get stubs.

## Background / Context

- DDD.md Section 5, TicketPlan aggregate: Contains GeneratedEpic, GeneratedTicket, DependencyOrder, TicketDetailLevel. 5 invariants defined.
- DDD.md Domain Story 5: describes the ticket pipeline flow — read DDD artifacts, generate one epic per BC, order by aggregate dependency, apply two-tier detail levels, preview, approve.
- Complexity Budget treatment levels from DDD.md Section 3: Core=full ticket detail, Supporting=standard, Generic=stub.
- CLI design: `docs/research/20260222_cli_mcp_design.md` — `alty generate tickets` maps to TicketGenerationPort, TicketPlan aggregate.
- Beads ticket template: `docs/beads_templates/beads-ticket-template.md` — defines the target structure for full-detail tickets (Goal, DDD Alignment, Design, SOLID Mapping, TDD Workflow, Steps, AC, Edge Cases, Quality Gates, Risks).
- Epic template: `docs/beads_templates/beads-epic-template.md` — defines the target structure for generated epics.
- **Pattern reference:** This ticket's own description is an example of "full detail" ticket output. The beads templates define the format.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Ticket Pipeline (CORE subdomain) |
| Ubiquitous Language | TicketPlan, GeneratedEpic, GeneratedTicket, DependencyOrder, TicketDetailLevel, Two-Tier Generation, TicketPlanApproved |
| Layer | Domain (TicketPlan aggregate + value objects) + Application (TicketGenerationHandler) + Infrastructure (beads writer adapter + CLI adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `TicketPlan` | Aggregate Root | Complete set of generated tickets for a project before approval — epics, tasks, dependency order |
| `GeneratedEpic` | Entity | One epic per bounded context: title, description, bounded_context_id, subdomain_classification |
| `GeneratedTicket` | Entity | A task within an epic: title, description (formatted per template), detail_level, dependencies, bounded_context_id |
| `DependencyOrder` | Value Object | Topologically sorted build sequence derived from aggregate dependencies and context relationships |
| `TicketDetailLevel` | Value Object (Enum) | FULL (Core), STANDARD (Supporting), STUB (Generic) — determines how much detail is generated |
| `TicketPlanApproved` | Domain Event | Emitted when approve() succeeds — carries approved ticket IDs and dismissed ticket IDs |

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI (alty generate tickets)   TicketGenerationHandler    TicketPlan        BeadsWriterPort
        |                            |                      |                  |
        |-- generate(model_data) --->|                      |                  |
        |                            |-- create_plan() ---->|                  |
        |                            |   For each BC:       |                  |
        |                            |   generate_epic() -->|                  |
        |                            |   For each aggregate:|                  |
        |                            |   generate_ticket()  |                  |
        |                            |   compute_order() -->|                  |
        |                            |<-- plan with order --|                  |
        |<-- preview() --------------|                      |                  |
        |   (show epics + tickets)   |                      |                  |
        |-- approve(ids) ----------->|                      |                  |
        |                            |-- approve() -------->|                  |
        |                            |<- TicketPlanApproved |                  |
        |                            |-- write_epics() ---------------------->|
        |                            |-- write_tickets() ------------------->|
        |<-- "Tickets created" ------|                      |                  |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | `TicketPlan` owns dependency ordering and invariants; `TicketGenerationHandler` orchestrates the pipeline; `TicketDetailRenderer` generates ticket descriptions per detail level; `BeadsWriterPort` writes to beads |
| **O**pen/Closed | New detail levels or ticket sections can be added by extending the TicketDetailLevel-to-sections mapping without modifying existing rendering logic |
| **L**iskov Substitution | `TicketGenerationPort` Protocol honored by CLI adapter and future MCP adapter |
| **I**nterface Segregation | `TicketGenerationPort` (generate + preview + approve), `BeadsWriterPort` (write tickets to beads) are separate Protocols |
| **D**ependency Inversion | `TicketGenerationHandler` depends on `BeadsWriterPort` Protocol, not on concrete beads CLI; domain logic computes the plan without knowing how tickets are persisted |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```python
# tests/domain/test_ticket_plan.py
def test_create_plan_empty():
    """New TicketPlan has no epics or tickets."""

def test_generate_plan_creates_one_epic_per_bc():
    """Invariant 1: Each bounded context produces exactly one epic."""

def test_foundation_aggregates_ordered_first():
    """Invariant 2: Aggregates with no dependencies appear first in DependencyOrder."""

def test_no_circular_dependencies():
    """Invariant 3: Circular dependency in ticket graph raises InvariantViolation."""

def test_core_tickets_have_full_detail():
    """Invariant 4: Core subdomain tickets must have FULL detail level with AC, TDD, SOLID, edge cases."""

def test_no_cross_bc_aggregate_references():
    """Invariant 5: Ticket referencing aggregate from another BC raises InvariantViolation."""

def test_supporting_tickets_have_standard_detail():
    """Supporting subdomain tickets have STANDARD detail level."""

def test_generic_tickets_are_stubs():
    """Generic subdomain tickets have STUB detail level."""

def test_dependency_order_topological_sort():
    """DependencyOrder is a valid topological sort of aggregate dependencies."""

def test_reorder_ticket():
    """Manual reorder adjusts position without violating dependency constraints."""

def test_reorder_violating_dependency_raises():
    """Reordering a ticket before its dependency raises DependencyViolation."""

def test_promote_stub_to_full():

def test_preview_returns_structured_summary():
    """Preview shows epic count, ticket count per detail level, dependency graph."""

def test_approve_subset():

def test_approve_emits_ticket_plan_approved():

# tests/domain/test_ticket_detail_renderer.py

```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_ticket_plan.py tests/domain/test_ticket_detail_renderer.py -v` — should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/ticket_values.py` — TicketDetailLevel enum (FULL, STANDARD, STUB), DependencyOrder value object
2. Create `src/domain/models/generated_epic.py` — GeneratedEpic entity with title, description, bounded_context_id, classification
3. Create `src/domain/models/generated_ticket.py` — GeneratedTicket entity with title, description, detail_level, dependencies, epic_id
4. Create `src/domain/models/ticket_plan.py` — TicketPlan aggregate root with generate_plan(), reorder(), promote_stub(), preview(), approve(), all 5 invariants. Includes topological sort for DependencyOrder.
5. Create `src/domain/services/ticket_detail_renderer.py` — Domain service that renders ticket descriptions per TicketDetailLevel, using beads template structure for FULL detail.
6. Create `src/domain/events/ticket_events.py` — TicketPlanApproved event
7. Create `src/application/commands/ticket_generation_handler.py` — orchestrates reading DomainModel, building TicketPlan, writing via port
8. Create `src/application/ports/ticket_generation_port.py` — TicketGenerationPort Protocol
9. Create `src/application/ports/beads_writer_port.py` — BeadsWriterPort Protocol for writing to beads issue tracker

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_ticket_plan.py tests/domain/test_ticket_detail_renderer.py -v` — should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify ticket descriptions match beads template structure exactly
- Ensure topological sort handles all graph shapes (linear, diamond, forest)
- Verify TicketPlan has no infrastructure dependencies
- Run all quality gates

## Steps

1. Define TicketDetailLevel enum and DependencyOrder value object in `src/domain/models/ticket_values.py`. DependencyOrder wraps a list of ticket IDs in topologically sorted order with a validate() method that checks for cycles.
2. Implement GeneratedEpic entity in `src/domain/models/generated_epic.py` — one per bounded context, carries the BC's classification and responsibility.
3. Implement GeneratedTicket entity in `src/domain/models/generated_ticket.py` — carries detail_level, dependencies (list of ticket IDs), and the rendered description.
4. Implement TicketPlan aggregate root in `src/domain/models/ticket_plan.py` — generate_plan() takes a DomainModel and produces epics+tickets. Topological sort computes DependencyOrder. Enforce all 5 invariants. Commands: generate_plan, reorder, promote_stub, preview, approve.
5. Implement TicketDetailRenderer domain service in `src/domain/services/ticket_detail_renderer.py` — renders ticket descriptions matching beads-ticket-template.md for FULL, abbreviated for STANDARD, minimal for STUB.
6. Define TicketPlanApproved event in `src/domain/events/ticket_events.py`.
7. Define Protocols: TicketGenerationPort, BeadsWriterPort.
8. Implement TicketGenerationHandler — reads DomainModel, builds TicketPlan, previews, writes approved tickets via BeadsWriterPort.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each bounded context produces exactly one epic (invariant 1)
- [ ] Foundation aggregates (no incoming dependencies) are ordered first in the build sequence (invariant 2)
- [ ] Circular dependencies in the ticket graph are detected and rejected (invariant 3)
- [ ] Core subdomain tickets have FULL detail: Goal, DDD Alignment, Design, SOLID Mapping, TDD Workflow (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR), Steps, AC, Edge Cases, Quality Gates, Risks (invariant 4)
- [ ] No ticket references an aggregate from a different bounded context except through defined context relationships (invariant 5)
- [ ] Supporting subdomain tickets have STANDARD detail: Goal, DDD Alignment, Steps, AC, Quality Gates
- [ ] Generic subdomain tickets are STUBS: Goal ("integrate X / verify boundary"), single AC
- [ ] DependencyOrder is a valid topological sort — every ticket appears after its dependencies
- [ ] reorder() respects dependency constraints — cannot move a ticket before its dependency
- [ ] promote_stub() converts a STUB ticket to FULL detail level with all sections populated
- [ ] Preview shows structured summary: epic count, ticket count per detail level, dependency graph
- [ ] approve() supports partial approval — can approve some tickets and dismiss others
- [ ] TicketPlanApproved event carries both approved and dismissed ticket IDs
- [ ] Generated ticket descriptions match the structure in docs/beads_templates/beads-ticket-template.md
- [ ] Domain layer has zero external dependencies
- [ ] >= 90% test coverage on domain layer, >= 80% overall

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Single BC, single aggregate | DomainModel with one BC and one aggregate | One epic, one ticket, no dependency ordering needed |
| Diamond dependency | A depends on B and C; B and C both depend on D | D first, then B and C (either order), then A |
| All Generic subdomains | Every BC classified as Generic | All tickets are stubs; warn "no Core subdomains — consider reclassifying" |
| Circular dependency detected | A -> B -> C -> A | Raise InvariantViolation("Circular dependency: A -> B -> C -> A") |
| Reorder before dependency | Move ticket A before its dependency B | Raise DependencyViolation("Cannot place A before B — A depends on B") |
| Promote stub with missing data | promote_stub() but DomainModel lacks aggregate details for this BC | Populate FULL template with available data; mark missing sections as "needs elaboration" |
| Empty DomainModel | No bounded contexts | Raise ValueError("No bounded contexts to generate tickets for") |
| Very large model | 10+ BCs, 30+ aggregates | Performance: topological sort must complete in < 1 second |
| Duplicate epic for same BC | Attempt to generate two epics for one BC | Raise InvariantViolation("BC 'X' already has an epic") |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes (zero errors)
- [ ] Type check passes (zero errors)
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% overall coverage
- [ ] Domain layer tests have >= 90% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- **Blocked by k7m.18** (artifact generation) — needs DomainModel with BoundedContextMap, AggregateDesigns, and SubdomainClassifications as input
- **Risk:** beads CLI API may have limitations for bulk ticket creation — use BeadsWriterPort Protocol to isolate from beads implementation details
- **Risk:** Topological sort edge cases (disconnected subgraphs, single-node graphs) — test all graph shapes explicitly
- **Risk:** Generated ticket descriptions may be too long for beads' description field — test with realistic DomainModel sizes
- **Dependency:** beads must support parent-child relationships (epic -> task) via `bd create --parent`
notes:
**DOGFOODING INSIGHT (2026-02-23):** We discovered that all 27 dependencies in the k7m epic were described in ticket text but never formally set with `bd dep add`. This made `bd blocked`/`bd ready`/ripple review blind to the dependency graph.

**REQUIREMENT for implementation:** The BeadsWriterPort MUST include dependency-setting as part of the write operation:
1. After creating tickets via `bd create`, call `bd dep add <ticket> <depends-on>` for each dependency from the DependencyOrder
2. Add an acceptance criterion: "All dependencies from DependencyOrder are formally set via bd dep add (not just described in text)"
3. Add a verification step: after writing all tickets, run `bd blocked` and confirm the blocked set matches expectations
4. The Risks/Dependencies section in generated ticket descriptions should include a warning that text-only deps are invisible to the tooling

title: Implement alty generate configs (multi-tool config generation)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Implement `alty generate configs` — the CLI command that generates tool-native configuration files (.claude/CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules, .roo/ and .roomodes, etc.) from the project's domain model. Currently, alty has hardcoded config templates in the bash `bin/alty` script. This ticket replaces that with a proper adapter-per-tool architecture where one domain model produces multiple tool-specific outputs via ConfigGenerationPort.

## Background / Context

- CLI design spike (k7m.4) defined `alty generate configs` mapping to the Tool Translation bounded context via `ConfigGenerationPort` (docs/research/20260222_cli_mcp_design.md, section 2).
- MCP equivalent: `generate_configs(project_dir, tools: list[str])` returning `ConfigResult`.
- The bash `bin/alty` script already has `setup_claude_code()`, `setup_cursor()`, `setup_roo_code()` functions (lines 466-541) — these contain the domain knowledge about each tool's config format that must be extracted into proper adapters.
- DDD.md section 4 (Tool Translation context) defines the ToolConfig aggregate, ToolAdapter entity, and ToolConvention value object.
- Blocked by k7m.18 (artifact generation) because configs reference the generated domain model (ubiquitous language, bounded contexts, agent personas).
- **Pattern reference:** Follow the port/adapter pattern established in k7m.13 (Typer CLI entry point).
- **k7m.1 finding:** `cross-tool/generation-matrix.toml` in `.alty/knowledge/` is the source of truth for what alty generates per tool. `ToolConvention` VO should read from `.alty/knowledge/tools/{tool}/current/` TOML entries for format details.
- **k7m.1 finding:** AGENTS.md is a cross-tool standard supported by Cursor, Roo Code, and OpenCode. alty should generate AGENTS.md as a common denominator file alongside tool-specific configs.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Tool Translation (Supporting subdomain) |
| Ubiquitous Language | ToolConfig, ToolAdapter, ToolConvention, ConfigsGenerated, Agent (persona config for AI tool) |
| Layer | Domain (ToolConfig aggregate, ToolConvention VO) + Application (ConfigGenerationHandler) + Infrastructure (file writers per tool + Typer CLI adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `ToolConfig` | Aggregate | Complete configuration for one AI tool — aggregate root |
| `ToolAdapter` | Entity | Translates domain model into a specific tool's config format (one per supported tool) |
| `ToolConvention` | Value Object | Known conventions for one tool version (config paths, format rules, required sections). Reads from `.alty/knowledge/tools/{tool}/current/` TOML entries. |
| `SupportedTool` | Value Object (Enum) | CLAUDE_CODE, CURSOR, ROO_CODE, OPENCODE |
| `ConfigsGenerated` | Domain Event | Emitted when tool configs are ready for preview |
| `ConfigSection` | Value Object | One section of a generated config (e.g., quality gates, agent instructions, DDD rules) |

### Generated Outputs Per Tool (from k7m.1 generation-matrix.toml)

| Tool | Generated Files |
|------|----------------|
| Claude Code | `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/agents/{persona}.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/rules/`, `.claude/commands/`, `.mcp.json` |
| Cursor | `AGENTS.md`, `.cursor/rules/{topic}.mdc` |
| Roo Code | `AGENTS.md`, `.roomodes`, `.roo/rules-{mode-slug}/`, `.roo/rules/` |
| OpenCode | `AGENTS.md`, `.opencode/agents/{persona}.md`, `.opencode/modes/{mode}.md`, `opencode.json`, `.opencode/rules/` |

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI (alty generate configs)     ConfigGenerationHandler     ToolConfig (Domain)     FileWriter (Infra)
    |                              |                           |                       |
    |-- generate(tools) ---------> |                           |                       |
    |                              |-- create_config(tool) --> |                       |
    |                              |    per tool in tools      |                       |
    |                              |                           |-- build_sections() -->|
    |                              |<-- ToolConfig[] --------- |                       |
    |                              |-- preview(configs) -----> |                       |
    |<-- ConfigPreview ----------- |                           |                       |
    |-- approve() ---------------> |                           |                       |
    |                              |-- write(configs) -------> |                       |
    |                              |                           |                  write files
    |<-- ConfigsGenerated -------- |                           |                       |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | `ToolConfig` owns config assembly; each `ToolAdapter` owns one tool's format; `FileWriter` owns disk output |
| **O**pen/Closed | New tools added by registering new `ToolAdapter` implementations — no modification to existing code |
| **L**iskov Substitution | All `ToolAdapter` implementations honor the `ToolAdapterProtocol` contract (accept DomainModel, return ConfigSections) |
| **I**nterface Segregation | `ConfigGenerationPort` has only `generate()` and `preview()`; `ToolAdapterProtocol` has only `translate(model) -> sections` |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Application layer depends on `ConfigGenerationPort` protocol; infrastructure provides `FileConfigWriter` and concrete adapters |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

Write failing tests first:

```python
# tests/domain/test_tool_config.py
def test_tool_config_builds_sections_from_domain_model():
    """ToolConfig should produce ConfigSections containing ubiquitous language and bounded context rules."""

def test_tool_config_rejects_unsupported_tool():
    """ToolConfig.create() raises ValueError for unknown tool type."""

def test_tool_convention_is_immutable():
    """ToolConvention value object cannot be mutated after creation."""

# tests/domain/test_tool_adapter.py
def test_claude_adapter_generates_claude_md_format():
    """Claude adapter produces .claude/CLAUDE.md with DDD layer rules and ubiquitous language."""

def test_cursor_adapter_generates_cursor_rules_format():
    """Cursor adapter produces .cursor/rules/ files with project conventions."""

def test_cursor_adapter_generates_agents_md():
    """Cursor adapter produces AGENTS.md as cross-tool standard."""

def test_roo_code_adapter_generates_roomodes():
    """Roo Code adapter produces .roomodes with custom mode definitions."""

def test_roo_code_adapter_generates_roo_rules():
    """Roo Code adapter produces .roo/rules/ and .roo/rules-{slug}/ files."""

def test_roo_code_adapter_generates_agents_md():
    """Roo Code adapter produces AGENTS.md as cross-tool standard."""

def test_opencode_adapter_generates_agents_md():
    """OpenCode adapter produces AGENTS.md as cross-tool standard."""

# tests/application/test_config_generation_handler.py
def test_handler_generates_configs_for_selected_tools():
    """Handler creates ToolConfig for each requested tool and returns preview."""

def test_handler_skips_tools_not_requested():
    """Handler only generates configs for tools in the request list."""

def test_handler_emits_configs_generated_event_on_approve():
    """After approval, handler emits ConfigsGenerated domain event."""
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_tool_config.py tests/domain/test_tool_adapter.py tests/application/test_config_generation_handler.py -v` -> should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/tool_config.py` — ToolConfig aggregate, ToolConvention VO, SupportedTool enum, ConfigSection VO
2. Create `src/domain/models/tool_adapter.py` — ToolAdapterProtocol and concrete adapters (ClaudeCodeAdapter, CursorAdapter, RooCodeAdapter, OpenCodeAdapter)
3. Create `src/domain/events/configs_generated.py` — ConfigsGenerated domain event
4. Create `src/application/commands/config_generation_handler.py` — ConfigGenerationHandler
5. Create `src/application/ports/config_generation_port.py` — ConfigGenerationPort protocol

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_tool_config.py tests/domain/test_tool_adapter.py tests/application/test_config_generation_handler.py -v` -> should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify ubiquitous language matches DDD.md section 4 (Tool Translation context)
- Ensure domain layer has zero imports from infrastructure or external libs
- Extract shared config sections (quality gates, TDD rules) into reusable ConfigSection factories
- Verify adapter registration is extensible (new tools can be added without modifying existing code)

## Steps

1. Define domain models — Create `ToolConfig` aggregate with `ToolConvention` VO, `SupportedTool` enum, and `ConfigSection` VO in `src/domain/models/tool_config.py`. ToolConfig.build_sections() assembles sections from a domain model reference.
2. Define tool adapters — Create `ToolAdapterProtocol` and concrete implementations (ClaudeCodeAdapter, CursorAdapter, RooCodeAdapter, OpenCodeAdapter) in `src/domain/models/tool_adapter.py`. Each adapter knows its tool's config format and translates domain model data into ConfigSections.
3. Define domain event — Create `ConfigsGenerated` event in `src/domain/events/configs_generated.py` with tool names and output paths.
4. Define application port — Create `ConfigGenerationPort` protocol in `src/application/ports/config_generation_port.py` with `generate(tools, domain_model)` and `preview(configs)` methods.
5. Implement handler — Create `ConfigGenerationHandler` in `src/application/commands/config_generation_handler.py` that coordinates adapter selection, config generation, preview, and approval flow.
6. Wire CLI adapter — Add `alty generate configs` Typer command in `src/infrastructure/cli/` that calls ConfigGenerationHandler (thin adapter, no business logic).
7. Implement file writer — Create `FileConfigWriter` in `src/infrastructure/persistence/` that writes generated configs to disk at tool-specific paths.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `alty generate configs` generates .claude/CLAUDE.md with project-specific DDD layer rules, ubiquitous language, and quality gates
- [ ] `alty generate configs` generates .cursor/rules/ files with equivalent project conventions
- [ ] `alty generate configs` generates AGENTS.md as cross-tool standard for Cursor, Roo Code, and OpenCode
- [ ] `alty generate configs` generates .roomodes and .roo/rules/ with custom mode definitions and project conventions
- [ ] `alty generate configs` generates .opencode/agents/ and opencode.json with equivalent configs
- [ ] Each adapter is independent — adding a new tool adapter requires no changes to existing adapters
- [ ] Preview mode shows what will be generated before writing files
- [ ] Generated configs include bounded context boundaries and ubiquitous language from DomainModel
- [ ] ConfigsGenerated event is emitted after successful generation
- [ ] ToolConvention VO reads format details from `.alty/knowledge/tools/{tool}/current/` TOML entries
- [ ] Domain layer (ToolConfig, ToolAdapter) has zero external dependencies
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage on new code

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No tools requested | `alty generate configs` with empty tool list | Error: "Specify at least one tool (claude, cursor, roo-code, opencode)" |
| Unknown tool name | `alty generate configs --tool unknown` | Error: "Unsupported tool: unknown. Supported: claude, cursor, roo-code, opencode" |
| Config already exists | Target .claude/CLAUDE.md already on disk | Follow file safety rules: skip or rename with _alty suffix |
| No domain model available | DomainModel not yet generated | Error: "Run alty generate artifacts first — domain model required" |
| Empty ubiquitous language | DomainModel has no terms defined | Generate config with placeholder section noting "No ubiquitous language defined yet" |
| Partial tool list | Only `--tool claude` requested | Generate only Claude Code config, skip others |
| AGENTS.md conflict | AGENTS.md already exists from another tool | Merge or warn — do not silently overwrite |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- **Blocked by k7m.18** (artifact generation) — configs reference the generated DomainModel. Can stub DomainModel for testing but full integration requires k7m.18.
- **Blocked by k7m.13** (Typer CLI entry point) — CLI adapter needs the Typer app scaffolding.
- Tool config formats may change — ToolConvention VO should be versioned (P1 scope, not this ticket).
- Roo Code and OpenCode config formats are documented in k7m.1 research report. Use `.alty/knowledge/tools/{tool}/current/` as the reference for format details.
- **Reference:** `cross-tool/generation-matrix.toml` from k7m.1 defines the complete set of files alty generates per tool.
notes: **PRD Traceability: C25** (Template-enforced ticket creation) — Generated CLAUDE.md must include grooming checklist with Template Compliance as step 1, and after-close protocol with template-enforced follow-up ticket creation as step 2. See prd-traceability.md C25.

title: Implement alty check (quality gate runner)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Implement `alty check` — the CLI command that runs quality gates (ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest tests, import-linter + pytestarch fitness functions). This is an orchestration command that delegates to external tools via subprocess and reports pass/fail results. Currently developers run each gate manually; `alty check` unifies them into a single command with selective flags.

## Background / Context

- CLI design spike (k7m.4) defined `alty check` mapping to Architecture Testing context via `QualityGatePort` (docs/research/20260222_cli_mcp_design.md, section 2).
- MCP equivalent: `check_quality(project_dir, gates: list[str])` returning `QualityResult`.
- PRD capability C13: "ruff + mypy + pytest enforced before ticket closure."
- CLI flags from spike: `--lint` (ruff check), `--types` (mypy), `--tests` (pytest), `--fitness` (import-linter + pytestarch). Run all if no flags specified.
- The pyproject.toml already has ruff, mypy, and pytest configured (from k7m.4 spike output).
- This is NOT a domain-heavy ticket — it is an Application + Infrastructure orchestration. The "domain" is minimal (gate definitions, pass/fail status). The value is in the clean port/adapter separation so MCP can reuse the same logic.
- **Pattern reference:** Follow port/adapter pattern from k7m.13 (Typer CLI entry point).

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Architecture Testing (CORE subdomain, but this command is the orchestration surface, not the fitness function generation) |
| Ubiquitous Language | QualityGate, GateResult, FitnessFunction (when --fitness is used) |
| Layer | Application (QualityGateHandler) + Infrastructure (SubprocessGateRunner + Typer CLI adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `QualityGate` | Value Object (Enum) | LINT, TYPES, TESTS, FITNESS — the four gate types |
| `GateResult` | Value Object | gate type + passed (bool) + output (str) + duration_ms (int) |
| `QualityReport` | Value Object | Collection of GateResults with overall pass/fail |

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI (alty check --lint --types)    QualityGateHandler       SubprocessGateRunner (Infra)
    |                                 |                           |
    |-- check(gates=[LINT,TYPES]) --> |                           |
    |                                 |-- run_gate(LINT) -------> |
    |                                 |                           |-- subprocess: ruff check .
    |                                 |<-- GateResult(LINT) ----- |
    |                                 |-- run_gate(TYPES) ------> |
    |                                 |                           |-- subprocess: mypy .
    |                                 |<-- GateResult(TYPES) ---- |
    |                                 |-- build_report() -------> |
    |<-- QualityReport -------------- |                           |
    |    (render pass/fail table)     |                           |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | `QualityGateHandler` orchestrates gate execution; `SubprocessGateRunner` runs subprocesses; CLI adapter renders output |
| **O**pen/Closed | New gates added by extending QualityGate enum and adding a runner mapping — no modification to handler logic |
| **L**iskov Substitution | `SubprocessGateRunner` implements `GateRunnerProtocol` — can be swapped for `MockGateRunner` in tests |
| **I**nterface Segregation | `QualityGatePort` has only `check(gates)` returning `QualityReport`; `GateRunnerProtocol` has only `run(gate) -> GateResult` |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Handler depends on `GateRunnerProtocol`, not on subprocess directly. CLI depends on `QualityGatePort`, not handler internals. |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

Write failing tests first:

```python
# tests/domain/test_quality_gate.py
def test_quality_gate_enum_has_four_gates():
    """QualityGate enum contains LINT, TYPES, TESTS, FITNESS."""

def test_gate_result_is_immutable():
    """GateResult value object cannot be mutated after creation."""

def test_quality_report_overall_pass_when_all_gates_pass():
    """QualityReport.passed is True only when all GateResults passed."""

def test_quality_report_overall_fail_when_any_gate_fails():
    """QualityReport.passed is False if any GateResult failed."""

# tests/application/test_quality_gate_handler.py
def test_handler_runs_all_gates_when_none_specified():
    """When no gates specified, handler runs LINT + TYPES + TESTS + FITNESS."""

def test_handler_runs_only_requested_gates():
    """When gates=[LINT, TYPES], handler runs only those two."""

def test_handler_returns_report_with_results_per_gate():
    """Handler returns QualityReport containing one GateResult per requested gate."""

def test_handler_continues_after_gate_failure():
    """If LINT fails, handler still runs remaining gates (reports all results)."""

# tests/infrastructure/test_subprocess_gate_runner.py
def test_runner_executes_ruff_for_lint_gate():
    """SubprocessGateRunner runs 'uv run ruff check .' for LINT gate."""

def test_runner_captures_output_on_failure():
    """When a gate fails, GateResult.output contains the tool's stderr/stdout."""
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_quality_gate.py tests/application/test_quality_gate_handler.py -v` -> should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/quality_gate.py` — QualityGate enum, GateResult VO, QualityReport VO
2. Create `src/application/ports/quality_gate_port.py` — QualityGatePort protocol and GateRunnerProtocol
3. Create `src/application/commands/quality_gate_handler.py` — QualityGateHandler
4. Create `src/infrastructure/external/subprocess_gate_runner.py` — SubprocessGateRunner implementing GateRunnerProtocol

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_quality_gate.py tests/application/test_quality_gate_handler.py -v` -> should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify value objects are truly immutable (frozen dataclass or similar)
- Ensure handler logic is pure — no subprocess calls, only GateRunnerProtocol calls
- Add Rich formatting for CLI output (pass/fail table with colors)
- Verify infrastructure tests use actual subprocess calls (integration-level)

## Steps

1. Define domain models — Create `QualityGate` enum, `GateResult` VO, and `QualityReport` VO in `src/domain/models/quality_gate.py`. QualityReport has a `passed` property that is True only when all gates pass.
2. Define application port — Create `QualityGatePort` protocol in `src/application/ports/quality_gate_port.py` with `check(gates: list[QualityGate]) -> QualityReport`. Also define `GateRunnerProtocol` with `run(gate: QualityGate) -> GateResult`.
3. Implement handler — Create `QualityGateHandler` in `src/application/commands/quality_gate_handler.py`. Handler iterates over requested gates, calls GateRunnerProtocol.run() for each, collects GateResults, and builds QualityReport. If no gates specified, runs all four.
4. Implement subprocess runner — Create `SubprocessGateRunner` in `src/infrastructure/external/subprocess_gate_runner.py`. Maps each QualityGate to its subprocess command: LINT -> `uv run ruff check .`, TYPES -> `uv run mypy .`, TESTS -> `uv run pytest`, FITNESS -> `uv run pytest tests/fitness/ -v` (or import-linter if configured).
5. Wire CLI adapter — Add `alty check` Typer command with `--lint`, `--types`, `--tests`, `--fitness` flags. Command is a thin adapter that maps flags to QualityGate list and calls handler.
6. Add Rich output — Format QualityReport as a colored table: green checkmark for pass, red X for fail, duration per gate, overall verdict.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `alty check` with no flags runs all four gates (lint, types, tests, fitness)
- [ ] `alty check --lint` runs only ruff check
- [ ] `alty check --types` runs only mypy
- [ ] `alty check --tests` runs only pytest
- [ ] `alty check --fitness` runs only fitness functions (import-linter/pytestarch)
- [ ] Multiple flags can be combined: `alty check --lint --types`
- [ ] Output shows pass/fail per gate with tool output on failure
- [ ] Exit code is non-zero if any gate fails
- [ ] Handler can be called from MCP server with same behavior (no CLI dependency)
- [ ] Domain models (QualityGate, GateResult, QualityReport) have zero external dependencies
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage on new code

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| All gates pass | Clean project | Exit 0, all green checkmarks |
| One gate fails | ruff finds lint error | Exit 1, show failing gate output, other gates still run |
| Tool not installed | mypy not in venv | GateResult(passed=False, output="mypy not found — install with: uv add mypy") |
| No pyproject.toml | Running in non-Python dir | Error: "No pyproject.toml found — run from project root" |
| Fitness tests missing | No tests/fitness/ directory | GateResult(passed=True, output="No fitness tests found — skipping") or skip gracefully |
| Subprocess timeout | Tool hangs | GateResult(passed=False, output="Timed out after 120s") — configurable timeout |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- **Blocked by k7m.13** (Typer CLI entry point) — needs the Typer app scaffolding for the CLI adapter.
- FITNESS gate depends on fitness function tests existing (from k7m.20 or similar). If no fitness tests exist, the gate should skip gracefully, not fail.
- Subprocess execution is inherently platform-dependent. Use `uv run` prefix for all tools to ensure venv activation. Test on Linux (primary platform).
- The `--fitness` flag implies import-linter config exists in pyproject.toml. If not configured, skip with informational message.

title: Implement alty kb (RLM knowledge base lookup)
description:
## Goal / Problem

Implement `alty kb <topic>` — the CLI command that looks up DDD patterns, tool conventions, and coding standards from the project's `.alty/knowledge/` directory. Topics use RLM-addressable paths like `ddd/aggregate`, `tools/claude-code/agent-format`, `conventions/solid`, `cross-tool/concept-mapping`. This gives AI agents and developers instant access to curated reference material without leaving the terminal.

## Background / Context

- CLI design spike (k7m.4) defined `alty kb <topic>` mapping to Knowledge Base context via `KnowledgeLookupPort` (docs/research/20260222_cli_mcp_design.md, section 2).
- MCP resource URIs: `alty://knowledge/ddd/{topic}`, `alty://knowledge/tools/{tool}/{subtopic}`, `alty://knowledge/conventions/{topic}`, `alty://knowledge/cross-tool/{topic}`.
- PRD capability C18: "Knowledge base (RLM) — Addressable docs for DDD patterns, coding tool conventions."
- **k7m.1 spike defines the complete directory structure** (Section 4) with TOML for tool conventions and Markdown for DDD/conventions reference:
  ```
  .alty/knowledge/
  ├── _index.toml                          # Master index for RLM O(1) lookup
  ├── tools/
  │   ├── claude-code/
  │   │   ├── _meta.toml                   # Tool metadata (name, URL, license, versions tracked)
  │   │   ├── current/                     # Alias -> latest tracked version (e.g., 2.1)
  │   │   │   ├── config-structure.toml
  │   │   │   ├── agent-format.toml
  │   │   │   ├── settings-format.toml
  │   │   │   ├── rules-format.toml
  │   │   │   ├── commands-format.toml
  │   │   │   ├── mcp-config.toml
  │   │   │   ├── global-paths.toml
  │   │   │   └── gitignore-patterns.toml
  │   │   ├── v2.1/                        # Explicit version (current alias target)
  │   │   ├── v2.0/                        # Previous major version
  │   │   └── v1.0/                        # Older major version
  │   ├── cursor/
  │   │   ├── _meta.toml
  │   │   └── current/
  │   │       ├── config-structure.toml
  │   │       ├── rules-format.toml
  │   │       ├── agents-md-support.toml
  │   │       ├── global-paths.toml
  │   │       └── gitignore-patterns.toml
  │   ├── roo-code/
  │   │   ├── _meta.toml
  │   │   └── current/
  │   │       ├── config-structure.toml
  │   │       ├── mode-format.toml
  │   │       ├── rules-format.toml
  │   │       ├── global-paths.toml
  │   │       └── gitignore-patterns.toml
  │   └── opencode/
  │       ├── _meta.toml
  │       └── current/
  │           ├── config-structure.toml
  │           ├── agent-format.toml
  │           ├── mode-format.toml
  │           ├── rules-format.toml
  │           ├── opencode-json-schema.toml
  │           ├── global-paths.toml
  │           └── gitignore-patterns.toml
  ├── cross-tool/                          # Cross-tool standards and mappings
  │   ├── agents-md.toml
  │   ├── concept-mapping.toml
  │   └── generation-matrix.toml
  ├── ddd/                                 # DDD patterns (Markdown, human consumption)
  │   ├── tactical-patterns.md
  │   ├── strategic-patterns.md
  │   ├── event-storming.md
  │   └── domain-storytelling.md
  └── conventions/                         # TDD, SOLID, quality gates (Markdown)
      ├── tdd.md
      ├── solid.md
      └── quality-gates.md
  ```
- DDD.md section 4 (Knowledge Base context) defines KnowledgeEntry entity and ToolVersion value object.
- Knowledge Base is a Supporting subdomain — standard architecture (simple layered), standard testing (>= 80%).
- **Pattern reference:** Follow port/adapter pattern from k7m.13 (Typer CLI entry point).
- **k7m.1 key design decision:** Tool convention entries are TOML (machine-parseable for ConfigGenerationPort and drift detection). DDD and convention entries remain Markdown (human consumption).

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Knowledge Base (Supporting subdomain) |
| Ubiquitous Language | KnowledgeEntry, ToolVersion, KnowledgeBase, RLM (Resource Locator for Markdown) |
| Layer | Domain (KnowledgeEntry entity) + Application (KnowledgeLookupHandler) + Infrastructure (FileKnowledgeReader + Typer CLI adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `KnowledgeEntry` | Entity | A single addressable document — has identity (path-based), contains title, content, metadata |
| `KnowledgePath` | Value Object | RLM address validating k7m.1 addressing scheme: `(category, tool_or_topic, subtopic, version?)`. Examples: `tools/claude-code/agent-format`, `ddd/tactical-patterns`, `cross-tool/concept-mapping` |
| `KnowledgeCategory` | Value Object (Enum) | DDD, TOOLS, CONVENTIONS, CROSS_TOOL — top-level categories (4 categories per k7m.1) |
| `EntryMetadata` | Value Object | Aligns with k7m.1 `[_meta]` schema: `last_verified`, `verified_against`, `confidence` (high/medium/low), `deprecated`, `next_review_date`, `schema_version`, `source_urls` |

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI (alty kb tools/claude-code/agent-format)    KnowledgeLookupHandler    FileKnowledgeReader (Infra)
    |                                               |                           |
    |-- lookup("tools/claude-code/agent-format") -> |                           |
    |                                               |-- resolve_path() -------> |
    |                                               |                           |-- read .alty/knowledge/tools/claude-code/current/agent-format.toml
    |                                               |<-- KnowledgeEntry ------- |
    |<-- formatted content ------------------------ |                           |
    |    (render TOML or Markdown)                  |                           |
```

**Note:** Tool entries resolve to `.toml` files; ddd/conventions entries resolve to `.md` files. The reader detects format by file extension.

### Version Parameter Support

```bash
alty kb tools/claude-code/agent-format                    # Resolves to current/ version
alty kb tools/claude-code/agent-format --version v2.0     # Resolves to v2.0/ version
alty kb ddd/tactical-patterns                             # No versioning (Markdown)
alty kb cross-tool/concept-mapping                        # No versioning (cross-tool)
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | `KnowledgeEntry` holds content and metadata; `KnowledgeLookupHandler` resolves paths and fetches entries; `FileKnowledgeReader` reads from disk |
| **O**pen/Closed | New knowledge categories added by placing files in `.alty/knowledge/<category>/` — no code changes needed |
| **L**iskov Substitution | `FileKnowledgeReader` implements `KnowledgeReaderProtocol` — can be swapped for test doubles or future DB-backed reader |
| **I**nterface Segregation | `KnowledgeLookupPort` has `lookup(category, topic, version)`, `list_tools()`, `list_versions(tool)`, `list_topics(category, tool?)` — focused interface per k7m.1 |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Handler depends on `KnowledgeReaderProtocol`, not on filesystem directly |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

Write failing tests first:

```python
# tests/domain/test_knowledge_entry.py
def test_knowledge_path_validates_format():
    """KnowledgePath('ddd/tactical-patterns') is valid; KnowledgePath('') raises ValueError."""

def test_knowledge_path_extracts_category():
    """KnowledgePath('ddd/tactical-patterns').category == KnowledgeCategory.DDD."""

def test_knowledge_path_extracts_category_cross_tool():
    """KnowledgePath('cross-tool/concept-mapping').category == KnowledgeCategory.CROSS_TOOL."""

def test_knowledge_path_extracts_topic():
    """KnowledgePath('tools/claude-code/agent-format').topic == 'claude-code/agent-format'."""

def test_knowledge_path_with_tool_subtopic():
    """KnowledgePath('tools/claude-code/agent-format') resolves tool='claude-code', subtopic='agent-format'."""

def test_knowledge_entry_has_identity():
    """Two KnowledgeEntry objects with same path are equal (entity identity)."""

def test_knowledge_entry_with_different_paths_are_not_equal():
    """KnowledgeEntry('ddd/tactical-patterns') != KnowledgeEntry('ddd/event-storming')."""

def test_entry_metadata_aligns_with_k7m1_schema():
    """EntryMetadata has fields: last_verified, verified_against, confidence, deprecated, next_review_date, schema_version, source_urls."""

# tests/application/test_knowledge_lookup_handler.py
def test_handler_returns_entry_for_valid_path():
    """Handler returns KnowledgeEntry when path resolves to existing file."""

def test_handler_raises_not_found_for_invalid_path():
    """Handler raises KnowledgeNotFoundError for non-existent topic."""

def test_handler_lists_topics_for_category():
    """Handler.list_topics('ddd') returns all topic names in ddd/ directory."""

def test_handler_lists_topics_for_tools_includes_subdirs():
    """Handler.list_topics('tools') returns ['claude-code', 'cursor', 'roo-code', 'opencode']."""

def test_handler_lists_topics_for_cross_tool():
    """Handler.list_topics('cross-tool') returns ['agents-md', 'concept-mapping', 'generation-matrix']."""

def test_handler_resolves_tool_entry_to_toml():
    """Tool entries resolve to .toml files (not .md)."""

def test_handler_resolves_ddd_entry_to_markdown():
    """DDD entries resolve to .md files."""

def test_handler_supports_version_parameter():
    """Handler resolves tools/claude-code/agent-format with version=v2.0 to v2.0/ subdir."""

def test_handler_defaults_to_current_version():
    """Handler resolves tools/claude-code/agent-format without version to current/ subdir."""
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_knowledge_entry.py tests/application/test_knowledge_lookup_handler.py -v` -> should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/knowledge_entry.py` — KnowledgeEntry entity, KnowledgePath VO (validates k7m.1 addressing scheme), KnowledgeCategory enum (DDD, TOOLS, CONVENTIONS, CROSS_TOOL), EntryMetadata VO (k7m.1 `[_meta]` schema)
2. Create `src/application/ports/knowledge_lookup_port.py` — KnowledgeLookupPort protocol and KnowledgeReaderProtocol
3. Create `src/application/queries/knowledge_lookup_handler.py` — KnowledgeLookupHandler (query handler, not command)
4. Create `src/infrastructure/persistence/file_knowledge_reader.py` — FileKnowledgeReader implementing KnowledgeReaderProtocol

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/test_knowledge_entry.py tests/application/test_knowledge_lookup_handler.py -v` -> should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify KnowledgePath validation covers all edge cases (trailing slashes, double slashes, etc.)
- Ensure KnowledgeEntry is a proper entity (identity by path, not by content)
- Add Rich markdown/TOML rendering for terminal output in CLI adapter
- Verify directory structure matches k7m.1 Section 4 layout

## Steps

1. Define domain models — Create `KnowledgeEntry` entity with `KnowledgePath` VO and `KnowledgeCategory` enum (DDD, TOOLS, CONVENTIONS, CROSS_TOOL) in `src/domain/models/knowledge_entry.py`. KnowledgePath validates k7m.1 addressing scheme: `(category, tool_or_topic, subtopic, version?)`. EntryMetadata aligns with k7m.1 `[_meta]` schema.
2. Define application port — Create `KnowledgeLookupPort` protocol in `src/application/ports/knowledge_lookup_port.py` with `lookup(category, topic, version)`, `list_tools()`, `list_versions(tool)`, `list_topics(category, tool?)`. Also define `KnowledgeReaderProtocol`.
3. Implement handler — Create `KnowledgeLookupHandler` in `src/application/queries/knowledge_lookup_handler.py`. Handler parses KnowledgePath, delegates to KnowledgeReaderProtocol, returns KnowledgeEntry or raises KnowledgeNotFoundError. Supports version parameter for tool entries.
4. Implement file reader — Create `FileKnowledgeReader` in `src/infrastructure/persistence/file_knowledge_reader.py`. Maps KnowledgePath to filesystem: tool entries -> `.alty/knowledge/tools/{tool}/{version}/{subtopic}.toml`, ddd/conventions -> `.alty/knowledge/{category}/{topic}.md`, cross-tool -> `.alty/knowledge/cross-tool/{topic}.toml`. Parses TOML `[_meta]` sections into EntryMetadata.
5. Wire CLI adapter — Add `alty kb <topic>` Typer command with optional `--version` parameter. Renders KnowledgeEntry content using Rich (markdown for .md, formatted TOML for .toml). If no topic given, show available categories. If category given without topic, list topics in that category.
6. Seed initial knowledge files — Create placeholder files in `.alty/knowledge/` for core topics using k7m.1 structure (TOML for tools, Markdown for ddd/conventions).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `alty kb ddd/tactical-patterns` displays the DDD tactical patterns reference document (Markdown)
- [ ] `alty kb tools/claude-code/agent-format` displays Claude Code agent format documentation (TOML)
- [ ] `alty kb tools/claude-code/agent-format --version v2.0` displays the v2.0 version of the entry
- [ ] `alty kb conventions/solid` displays SOLID principles reference (Markdown)
- [ ] `alty kb cross-tool/concept-mapping` displays the cross-tool concept mapping (TOML)
- [ ] `alty kb` with no argument lists all available categories (ddd, tools, conventions, cross-tool)
- [ ] `alty kb ddd` lists all topics in the ddd category
- [ ] `alty kb tools` lists all tool names (claude-code, cursor, roo-code, opencode)
- [ ] `alty kb tools/claude-code` lists all subtopics for Claude Code
- [ ] `alty kb nonexistent/topic` shows clear error: "Topic not found: nonexistent/topic"
- [ ] KnowledgePath validates against path traversal (../../etc/passwd rejected)
- [ ] KnowledgeCategory enum includes CROSS_TOOL
- [ ] EntryMetadata includes k7m.1 fields: last_verified, verified_against, confidence, deprecated, next_review_date, schema_version, source_urls
- [ ] Tool entries resolve to TOML files; ddd/conventions resolve to Markdown files
- [ ] MCP resource URI `alty://knowledge/tools/claude-code/agent-format` maps to same content (via shared handler)
- [ ] Domain models (KnowledgeEntry, KnowledgePath) have zero external dependencies
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage on new code

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Empty topic | `alty kb ""` | List all categories |
| Category only | `alty kb ddd` | List topics in ddd/ directory |
| Tool name only | `alty kb tools/claude-code` | List subtopics for claude-code (agent-format, config-structure, etc.) |
| Tool with version | `alty kb tools/claude-code/agent-format --version v2.0` | Resolve to .alty/knowledge/tools/claude-code/v2.0/agent-format.toml |
| Version not found | `alty kb tools/claude-code/agent-format --version v99.0` | Error: "Version v99.0 not found for claude-code. Available: current, v2.1, v2.0, v1.0" |
| Cross-tool entry | `alty kb cross-tool/generation-matrix` | Resolve to .alty/knowledge/cross-tool/generation-matrix.toml |
| Path traversal | `alty kb ../../etc/passwd` | ValueError: "Invalid knowledge path" |
| Missing .alty/ | No knowledge directory | Error: "Knowledge base not found — run alty init first" |
| File exists but empty | Empty .toml or .md file | KnowledgeEntry with empty content, no crash |
| Unicode topic name | `alty kb ddd/agregat` (typo) | KnowledgeNotFoundError with "Did you mean: ddd/tactical-patterns?" (fuzzy match, stretch goal) |
| _meta.toml lookup | `alty kb tools/claude-code/_meta` | Return tool metadata (name, versions tracked, etc.) |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- **Blocked by k7m.13** (Typer CLI entry point) — needs the Typer app scaffolding for the CLI adapter.
- Knowledge content files need to be authored separately. This ticket creates the lookup infrastructure and placeholder files, not the full knowledge base content.
- Fuzzy matching on typos is a stretch goal — implement exact match first, add fuzzy later if time permits.
- MCP resource integration depends on k7m.14 (MCP server setup) — the handler is shared but MCP wiring is separate.
- **k7m.1 is the source of truth** for directory structure, TOML schemas, and `[_meta]` fields. Refer to docs/research/20260222_knowledge_base_structure.md for implementation details.

title: Migrate alty doc-health from bash to Python/Typer
description:
## Goal / Problem

Migrate `alty doc-health` from the existing bash implementation (bin/alty, lines 893-966) to a Python/Typer command. The bash version checks three hardcoded docs (PRD.md, DDD.md, ARCHITECTURE.md) for freshness via `last_reviewed` frontmatter and scans docs/ for missing frontmatter. The Python version must preserve this behavior while adding: configurable doc registry (`.alty/maintenance/doc-registry.toml`), structured output via DocHealthPort, and Rich terminal formatting.

## Background / Context

- CLI design spike (k7m.4) defined `alty doc-health` mapping to Knowledge Base context via `DocHealthPort` (docs/research/20260222_cli_mcp_design.md, section 2).
- MCP equivalent: `doc_health(project_dir)` returning `DocHealthResult`.
- PRD capability C19: "Doc maintenance commands — doc health, architecture lookup, knowledge refresh."
- PRD section 5.3: "Markdown files are living documents. alty doc-health checks freshness, broken references, missing metadata."
- Existing bash behavior (bin/alty `cmd_doc_health` function):
  - Checks docs/PRD.md, docs/DDD.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md for existence
  - Extracts `last_reviewed` from YAML frontmatter
  - Reports MISSING, STALE (no date or > 30 days), or OK (with days-since)
  - Scans all .md files in docs/ for missing frontmatter (INFO level)
  - Skips templates/ and beads_templates/ directories
  - Shows summary: "N issue(s) found across M docs"
- The `.alty/maintenance/doc-registry.toml` file (from PRD section 5.1) should define which docs to track, their owners, and review intervals — replacing the hardcoded doc list.
- Knowledge Base is a Supporting subdomain — standard detail.
- **Pattern reference:** Follow port/adapter pattern from k7m.13. The bash `cmd_doc_health` function is the behavioral spec.

## Knowledge Health Mode (from k7m.1 spike)

In addition to doc freshness checking, `alty doc-health` supports a `--knowledge` mode (`alty doc-health --knowledge`) that reads `[_meta]` sections from `.alty/knowledge/` TOML entries and reports knowledge entry staleness. This mode uses three staleness signals:

- **Time-based:** `now > next_review_date` (from `[_meta].next_review_date`)
- **Version mismatch:** `[_meta].verified_against` does not match the currently installed tool/library version
- **Confidence decay:** entries with `confidence = "low"` are flagged sooner for review

This is a **related but distinct** feature from the existing doc freshness scope. Doc freshness is time-based for PRD/DDD/ARCH files using YAML frontmatter `last_reviewed`. Knowledge freshness is per-entry TOML metadata from the `.alty/knowledge/` directory structure. Both are surfaced through `alty doc-health` but via different flags and different scanning logic.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Knowledge Base (Supporting subdomain) |
| Ubiquitous Language | DocHealthReport, DocStatus (OK/STALE/MISSING), last_reviewed, review interval, doc registry |
| Layer | Application (DocHealthHandler) + Infrastructure (FilesystemDocScanner + Typer CLI adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `DocHealthReport` | Value Object | Collection of DocStatus results with issue count and total checked |
| `DocStatus` | Value Object | One doc's health: path, status (OK/STALE/MISSING/NO_FRONTMATTER), last_reviewed date, days_since, review_interval |
| `DocHealthStatus` | Value Object (Enum) | OK, STALE, MISSING, NO_FRONTMATTER |
| `DocRegistryEntry` | Value Object | path, owner, review_interval_days — from doc-registry.toml |

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI (alty doc-health)       DocHealthHandler        FilesystemDocScanner (Infra)
    |                          |                           |
    |-- check() ------------> |                           |
    |                          |-- load_registry() ------> |
    |                          |                           |-- read .alty/maintenance/doc-registry.toml
    |                          |<-- DocRegistryEntry[] --- |
    |                          |-- scan_docs(registry) --> |
    |                          |                           |-- for each registered doc:
    |                          |                           |     check existence
    |                          |                           |     parse frontmatter
```
